basic_details.job_title: TNSRLM Resource Person Recruitment 2026 - Apply Offline for 181 State Resource Person, District Resource Person, Block Resource Person Posts
basic_details.short_information: Tamil Nadu State Rural Livelihoods Mission (TNSRLM) is inviting applications from eligible female candidates for the positions of State Resource Persons (SRPs), District Resource Persons (DRPs), and Block Resource Persons (BRPs) under the PRI-CBO Convergence Universalization - Phase II programme, now expanded to 15 new districts across Tamil Nadu. A total of 161 BRP positions are available, along with SRP and DRP posts. The last date to apply - by post or in person - is 29 June 2026. Applications must be submitted to the Project Director, District Mission Management Unit (DMMU), TNSRLM, of the

vacancy_details.Requirement: Post Graduate degree in Social Work / Sociology / Rural Development / Women Studies or any other Social Science stream
Minimum 10 years of experience working with SHGs / Federations, as a Trainer, in the NGO sector, or in Government rural development projects
Proficiency in reading and writing Tamil and English
Strong communication, coordination, interpersonal skills; proficiency in mobile apps, MS Word, MS Excel, and web portals
Post Graduate degree in Social Work / Sociology / Rural Development / Women Studies or any other Social Science stream
Minimum 4 years of experience working with SHGs / Federations, as a Trainer, in the NGO sector, or in Government rural development projects
Proficiency in reading and writing Tamil and English
Strong communication, coordination, interpersonal skills; proficiency in mobile apps, MS Word, MS Excel, and web portals
Under Graduate degree in Social Work / Sociology / Rural Development / Women Studies or any other Social Science stream
Minimum 3 years of experience working with SHGs / Federations, as a Trainer, in the NGO sector, or in Government rural development projects
Proficiency in reading and writing Tamil and English
Strong communication, coordination, interpersonal skills; proficiency in mobile apps, MS Word, MS Excel, and web portals

selection_process: 2 Micro Teaching cum Interview - Communication Skill 10 Marks

marks-based selection process that differs between the state/district-level posts and the block-level posts. Candidates should prepare for both a written test and a practical/interview component - the selection is competitive and tests both knowledge and teaching ability.
Also Read: MAHATRANSCO Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 77 Posts
how_to_apply: Unlike most government recruitment processes, the TNSRLM PRI-CBO Phase II application must be submitted physically - either by post or in person - to the Project Director of the DMMU in the district the candidate is applying for. There is no online application portal for this recruitment. Applications reaching after 29 June 2026 will not be considered.
Identify the district you wish to apply for from the 15 notified districts
Prepare your application with all relevant personal, educational, and experience details
Attach self-attested copies of all supporting documents (educational certificates, experience certificates, SHG membership proof if applicable, age proof, and caste certificate if applicable)
Address the application envelope clearly to the Project Director, District Mission Management Unit (TNSRLM) of the respective district
Submit the application either by post or in person at the DMMU address before 29 June 2026
Ensure the application reaches the DMMU on or before the due date - no extensions will be granted

faq.question: Q1. Has TNSRLM officially released the SRP, DRP, and BRP Recruitment Notification for PRI-CBO Phase II?
faq.answer: Yes. Tamil Nadu State Rural Livelihoods Mission (TNSRLM) has officially released the recruitment notification for State Resource Persons (SRPs), District Resource Persons (DRPs), and Block Resource Persons (BRPs) under PRI-CBO Convergence Universalization – Phase II, covering 15 new districts across Tamil Nadu. The last date to apply is 29 June 2026.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available under TNSRLM PRI-CBO Phase II?
faq.answer: TNSRLM has notified 161 BRP positions assigned across blocks in 15 districts, along with SRP positions at the rate of 1 per every 3 districts and DRP positions at 1 per district. The total SRP and DRP counts are determined by the district empanelment ratio specified in the notification.

faq.question: Q3. What is the last date to apply for TNSRLM SRP, DRP, and BRP 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit applications - either by post or in person - to the Project Director, DMMU, TNSRLM, of the concerned district is 29 June 2026. Applications received after this date will not be entertained under any circumstances.

faq.question: Q4. What is the educational qualification required for each post?
faq.answer: For SRP and DRP, a Post Graduate degree in Social Work, Sociology, Rural Development, Women Studies, or any Social Science stream is required. For BRP, an Under Graduate degree in the same streams is sufficient. All posts require proficiency in Tamil and English.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for TNSRLM SRP, DRP, and BRP?
faq.answer: For SRP and DRP, the age limit is 30 to 45 years as on 01.06.2026. For BRP, the age limit is 25 to 45 years as on 01.06.2026. The notification does not specify a general age relaxation category beyond the lower age threshold difference.

faq.question: Q6. How much is the monthly honorarium for each post?
faq.answer: The anticipated maximum monthly honorarium is ₹25,000 for SRPs, ₹18,000 for DRPs, and ₹10,500 for BRPs — all calculated for up to 25 days of engagement per month. Travel allowance is paid separately at ₹250/day (SRP), ₹120/day (DRP), and ₹60/day (BRP).

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for TNSRLM SRP and DRP posts?
faq.answer: SRP and DRP selection involves three stages: a Written Test (50 marks), a Focus Group Discussion (10 marks), and a Micro Teaching cum Interview (40 marks), for a total of 100 marks. Final selection is made on the basis of combined scores across all three stages.

faq.question: Q8. What is the selection process for TNSRLM BRP posts?
faq.answer: BRP selection has two stages totalling 100 marks: a Written Test with MCQs (50 marks) and a Micro Teaching cum Interview (50 marks). The interview component assesses communication skill, teaching skill, subject knowledge, computer knowledge, and training experience - 10 marks each.

faq.question: Q9. Is this recruitment open to male candidates?
faq.answer: No. All three posts - SRP, DRP, and BRP - are open to female candidates only. The notification states a preference for candidates who are SHG (Self Help Group) members, though this is preferred and not strictly mandatory.

faq.question: Q10. Which 15 districts are covered under TNSRLM PRI-CBO Phase II?
faq.answer: The 15 districts covered are: Ariyalur, Chengalpattu, Cuddalore, Kancheepuram, Krishnagiri, Madurai, Mayiladuthurai, Nagapattinam, Namakkal, Sivagangai, Tenkasi, Tiruvallur, Tiruvannamalai, Tiruvarur, and Villupuram.

faq.answer: Yes. Tamil Nadu State Rural Livelihoods Mission (TNSRLM) has officially released the recruitment notification for State Resource Persons (SRPs), District Resource Persons (DRPs), and Block Resource Persons (BRPs) under PRI-CBO Convergence Universalization – Phase II, covering 15 new districts across Tamil Nadu.
